alert.message: Resource failure: Failed to pull image "nginx:BROKEN-221830": rpc error: code = NotFound desc = failed to pull and unpack image "docker.io/library/nginx:BROKEN-221830": failed to resolve reference "docker.io/library/nginx:BROKEN-221830": docker.io/library/nginx:BROKEN-221830: not found

rca.root_cause: The deployment references a non-existent container image tag 'nginx:BROKEN-221830' which does not exist in the Docker Hub registry. The image pull fails with a 'not found' error, preventing the pod from starting. This is likely a misconfigured image tag introduced via a recent ArgoCD GitOps commit or Helm values update.
rca.recommended_action: Rollback the checkout-service deployment to the last known good revision using ArgoCD, which will restore the previous valid image tag. Then investigate the Git commit that introduced the 'BROKEN-221830' tag and fix the image reference in the GitOps repository.
rca.reasoning_trace: 1. The alert clearly indicates an image pull failure with 'not found' for tag 'nginx:BROKEN-221830'. 2. The tag name 'BROKEN-221830' is obviously not a valid nginx image tag — it appears to be a test/broken tag accidentally committed. 3. The pod cannot start without a valid image, so this is a deployment-blocking issue. 4. The safest and fastest remediation is to rollback to the previous known-good revision via ArgoCD, which will restore the working image tag. 5. Risk is low because rollback restores a previously working state. 6. Auto-remediation is safe here as it's a straightforward rollback with no data impact.

runbook_steps: Check ArgoCD app status: argocd app get <app-name> --insecure
Review sync error details in ArgoCD UI or: argocd app get <app-name> -o json | jq .status.conditions
Force hard refresh to pick up latest Git state: argocd app get --hard-refresh <app-name>
Trigger force sync: argocd app sync <app-name> --force --insecure
Monitor sync status: argocd app wait <app-name> --sync --insecure
Verify all resources are healthy: argocd app get <app-name> | grep Health
If sync fails, check for CRD issues or RBAC: kubectl describe events -n <namespace>
plan.approval_reason: Runbook retrieval confidence is low — top match is ambiguous. A wrong runbook executed confidently is worse than no runbook.. Please approve or reject this remediation.

escalation.summary: The `checkout-service` deployment is failing to start due to a `NotFound` error when pulling the non-existent image `nginx:BROKEN-221830` from Docker Hub. Execute the `deployment-failure-force-sync` runbook to immediately rollback the ArgoCD deployment to the last stable revision (~60s ETA, low risk). Post-recovery, audit the recent GitOps/Helm commit to correct the invalid image reference and prevent recurrence.
escalation.recommended_action: Rollback the checkout-service deployment to the last known good revision using ArgoCD, which will restore the previous valid image tag. Then investigate the Git commit that introduced the 'BROKEN-221830' tag and fix the image reference in the GitOps repository.
